title: Symantec Endpoint Protection Security Events and Device Control Activity
app: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection (Splunkbase 2772)
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:security:file" earliest=-24h
| eval host=coalesce(Computer_Name, computer_name, host)
| eval desc=coalesce(Event_Description, event_description, _raw)
| where match(lower(desc), "(?i)device control|application control|usb|firewall|tamper")
| stats count by host, desc
| sort -count
description: Security client events referencing device control, application control, USB, firewall, or tamper protection show policy enforcement actions that often precede insider misuse or malware attempting to disable protection.
value: Centralizes SEP policy violation narratives that are easy to miss in SEPM consoles yet critical for insider-threat and ransomware resilience programs.
implementation: Enable Client Security log collection in SEPM exports. Tune the `match` expression to your organization’s localized `Event_Description` strings. Correlate spikes with change windows when new device control rules deploy. For high-volume environments, summarize hourly before alerting.
visualization: Bar chart (top descriptions), table (host × description), timeline.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection
About the Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection
